title: Ensuring Halal Compliance Through Routine qPCR Surveillance
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Halal   integrity     is     a     growing     priority   in global   food     systems  ,   particularly     for   Muslim-  majority     markets     and     international     trade  .   Routine     qPCR     surveillance     provides     a     robust  ,   rapid  ,   and     reliable     method     for     verifying     the     authenticity     of     animal-derived     food     products     and     detecting   non-halal   adulterants  . By   targeting     species-specific   DNA   markers  ,   qPCR     enables     precise     identification     of     prohibited     materials  ,   such   as   porcine     derivatives  ,   even     at     trace     levels  . The   implementation     of     qPCR-based   halal   monitoring     strengthens     consumer     confidence  ,   prevents     fraud  ,   and     ensures     compliance     with     religious     and     regulatory     standards  .   Integrating     qPCR     into   halal   assurance     systems     supports     transparency     across     the     supply     chain     and     helps     producers     meet     domestic     and     export     requirements  ,   fostering     market     competitiveness     and     reinforcing     public     trust   in   certified   halal   products  .
